step_by_step_thinking:

Based on the gathered information:
- Multiple studies indicate that tetracycline-induced tooth discoloration generally begins around 20-50 mg/kg/day depending upon the duration of treatment and susceptibility of the child.
- Several references mention that lower doses like 5 mg/kg might still pose some risk though not consistently reported everywhere suggesting higher likelihood above certain thresholds.
- Consistent emphasis was placed on avoidance especially below 8 years old due to ongoing dentition formation.

raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("minimum dosage tetracycline tooth discoloration")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered information:
- Multiple studies indicate that tetracycline-induced tooth discoloration generally begins around 20-50 mg/kg/day depending upon the duration of treatment and susceptibility of the child.
- Several references mention that lower doses like 5 mg/kg might still pose some risk though not consistently reported everywhere suggesting higher likelihood above certain thresholds.
- Consistent emphasis was placed on avoidance especially below 8 years old due to ongoing dentition formation.
Given this detailed review, my revised stance aligns more closely now supporting Option **\boxed{B}** since there seems sufficient scientific basis showing even modest increases beyond baseline recommendations start posing notable risks sooner rather than later. Hence, considering conservative approach would favor cautionary threshold set closer to midpoint suggested here i.e., 20mg/Kg mark. Agreeing thusly reinforces original assessment made albeit refining edge slightly downward toward safer side presuming full adherence to guidelines outlined herein.
